title: Managed Web/API Hosting on Free/Low-Cost PaaS
description: Deploy APIs and background workers on free/low-cost PaaS/managed hosting providers (e.g., Render/Railway/Fly.io) to reduce ops; validate sleeping behavior, quotas, and SLA/availability targets.

cost.costNotes: Estimates are intentionally conservative; verify against current pricing before committing. Free tiers are quota-limited; production use may require paid plans and compliance agreements.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Min $0 represents free tier usage across providers (Render free instances with 750hrs/month, Railway $5 credit, Fly.io 3 shared-cpu VMs free). Max $150 represents low-cost production usage: 2-3 paid instances on Render ($7-21/month each for starter instances), Railway hobby tier ($20-50/month with usage), or Fly.io paid instances ($1.94-5.70/month per VM). This range covers hobby/side projects to small production APIs with moderate traffic. Enterprise production workloads would exceed this range but fall outside the 'low-cost PaaS' pattern scope.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-low complexity adoption. Requires: (1) Platform evaluation and selection (4-8 hours comparing Render/Railway/Fly.io features, pricing, SLAs), (2) Application containerization/buildpack setup (8-12 hours for proper production configuration), (3) CI/CD pipeline setup (4-8 hours integrating GitHub/GitLab), (4) Environment variable and secrets management (2-4 hours), (5) Monitoring and logging configuration (4-6 hours), (6) Sleep behavior testing and cold-start optimization (4-6 hours critical for free tiers), (7) Backup and disaster recovery planning (4-6 hours). Total 30-50 hours at $50-80/hour blended rate (considering junior/mid-level developers can handle with senior oversight). Lower than self-managed infrastructure but requires PaaS-specific expertise.
provenance.licenseCost: No license costs. All mentioned PaaS providers (Render, Railway, Fly.io, DigitalOcean App Platform) operate on pay-as-you-go consumption models with free tiers and no upfront license fees. Costs are purely usage-based (compute hours, bandwidth, storage). No proprietary software licenses, SDK fees, or platform access charges required. Open-source application frameworks and tooling typically used with these platforms are also license-free.

provides.reasoning: This pattern specifically provides managed hosting infrastructure for APIs and web applications on PaaS platforms, eliminating the need to manage underlying infrastructure.
PaaS providers handle all infrastructure management including servers, networking, and OS maintenance, which is the core value proposition of this pattern.
Most PaaS platforms (Render, Railway, Fly.io) include automatic scaling capabilities as a standard feature of their managed hosting.
The pattern explicitly mentions deploying background workers alongside APIs on these PaaS platforms as a distinct hosting capability.

requires.reasoning: Most modern PaaS providers (Render, Railway, Fly.io) require applications to be containerized or follow specific buildpack conventions as a prerequisite for deployment.
Free/low-cost PaaS often involves sleeping instances and horizontal scaling, which requires stateless application design. This is critical given the pattern's mention of validating sleeping behavior.
Since PaaS instances can sleep or restart, the pattern requires external databases or storage services rather than relying on local disk persistence.
Free-tier PaaS services involve sleeping and waking instances, requiring applications to tolerate cold start delays and design for this behavior.
PaaS platforms require health check endpoints for monitoring instance health and managing sleep/wake behavior effectively.

supports_nfr.reason: Free/low-cost PaaS tiers lack enterprise SLAs and have auto-sleeping, limiting availability to ~99%.
Managed PaaS platforms on shared infrastructure introduce network overhead and cold starts.
Tail latencies on shared infrastructure with cold starts and network variability require minimum 200ms.
Managed PaaS platforms provide automated healing but require time to detect failures and restart.
Free/low-cost PaaS relies on external persistence with eventual consistency and replication lag.
Free/low-cost tiers have CPU, memory, and concurrency limits that cap throughput to ~10K jobs/hour.
Free/low-cost PaaS shared infrastructure and resource limits cap read QPS to ~1000.
Free/low-cost PaaS CPU and memory constraints limit write QPS to ~500 for stateless APIs.
PaaS providers support GDPR-compliant deployments through region selection and data residency controls.
Some PaaS providers offer HIPAA-compliant tiers; pattern is compatible with HIPAA requirements when configured.
PaaS hosting is compatible with CCPA compliance requirements when configured appropriately.
PaaS platforms can support SOX compliance through audit trails and access controls.

supports_constraints.reason: PaaS providers abstract cloud infrastructure and run on multiple clouds, supporting cloud-agnostic deployments.
PaaS providers support containerized deployments with broad language compatibility.
PaaS providers host backend services including APIs, web frontends, and background workers.
PaaS hosting is compatible with applications using caching or not.
PaaS platforms can host applications with or without async messaging capabilities.
PaaS hosting supports AI inference workloads within compute and memory limits.
PaaS hosting is compatible with multi-tenant and single-tenant application architectures.
Free-tier PaaS has runtime limits and auto-sleeping that make long-running batch jobs infeasible.
Distributed transactions require stable, always-available infrastructure incompatible with auto-sleeping PaaS.
Real-time streaming requires always-on infrastructure incompatible with auto-sleeping free-tier PaaS.
PaaS hosting can integrate with external vector databases for vector search capabilities.
PaaS hosting integrates with external document stores for persistence needs.
PaaS hosting integrates with external key-value stores for fast lookups.
PaaS hosting integrates with external graph databases for relationship queries.
PaaS hosting integrates with external time-series databases for temporal data.
PaaS hosting supports OLTP workloads through stateless APIs backed by external databases.
PaaS hosting can serve OLAP queries through external analytical databases within resource limits.

provider.description: PaaS provider selection. Render offers generous free tier with auto-sleep. Railway has simple pricing and excellent DX. Fly.io provides edge deployment globally. DigitalOcean App Platform offers predictable pricing.

instance_type.description: Service tier selection. Free tier has sleep/quota limits but zero cost. Starter provides always-on with basic resources. Standard offers production-grade resources and SLA.

auto_sleep.description: Allow instance to sleep after inactivity (free tier requirement). Reduces costs but adds cold-start latency. Disable for always-on availability on paid tiers.

health_check_enabled.description: Enable health check endpoints for automatic restarts and monitoring. Recommended for production but may consume free tier quotas.

scaling_strategy.description: Scaling approach. Manual keeps costs predictable. Horizontal auto-scaling adds instances under load (higher cost). Vertical auto-scaling adjusts resources per instance.

warn_nfr.reason: Free/low-cost PaaS shared resources and CPU limits make high-throughput workloads (>500 QPS) sub-optimal; dedicated infrastructure is more cost-effective at scale.
warn_nfr.message: hosting-managed-paas: peak read QPS is {actual} (>500 req/s). Free/low-cost PaaS resource limits make high-throughput workloads inefficient. Consider dedicated hosting or upgrading to higher-tier plans.

warn_nfr.reason: Write operations are CPU/memory intensive; free/low-cost PaaS tiers become cost-inefficient above ~200 writes/sec compared to dedicated infrastructure.
warn_nfr.message: hosting-managed-paas: peak write QPS is {actual} (>200 req/s). Free/low-cost PaaS CPU and memory limits make high write throughput sub-optimal. Consider dedicated infrastructure for better cost efficiency.
